tutorialscreen_image_rideage_1_old:
Ride Reputation is one of the main contributing factors of a ride's Prestige.


A ride's Reputation changes over time. Guests will start out excited for a New ride, but its popularity will lower as it becomes Dated.
tutorialscreen_image_rideage_2: After being Dated for a while, a ride's Reputation will then start Resurging. This will continue until it eventually becomes Old, where guests look for newer, more exciting rides.
tutorialscreen_image_rideage_3:
However, if a ride has high Prestige, instead of becoming Old, it turns into a Classic. At this point, the ride's popularity soars again and will stand the test of time.


Tip! Both Coasters and Flat Rides can attain Classic status - check how to improve their Prestige in the Prestige tab to achieve this!
tutorialscreen_image_ridecustomisation_1: You can attach scenery items to ride parts and coasters cars. This gives you great creative control over your rides and even increases their Scenery Scores!
tutorialscreen_image_ridecustomisation_2: The Twinning setting allows scenery to be duplicated automatically.
tutorialscreen_image_ridecustomisation_3: The Symmetry setting allows you to easily place duplicate scenery in symmetrical locations.
tutorialscreen_image_roundingtool_1: Some shapes have sharp corners. These can be adjusted with the Rounding Tool, to control how sharp or soft a corner is.
tutorialscreen_image_roundingtool_2: The power of the Rounding Tool can be adjusted with the Strength setting.
tutorialscreen_image_roundingtool_3: Shapes placed next to each other will share corners. This will also affect how the Rounding Tool smooths a corner.
tutorialscreen_image_scenerypark_1: Scenery plays an important part in the park experience. More Scenery means higher Prestige for attractions, an improved Guest Experience, and a higher Park Rating.
tutorialscreen_image_scenerypark_2: Scenery provides a Scenery Bonus to an attraction's Prestige. Attractions with a higher Prestige will attract more Guests, and Guests will be willing to pay higher ticket prices for them.
tutorialscreen_image_scenerypark_3:
Scenery Rating is one of four pillars that contribute to your Park Rating. Place more Scenery throughout your park to increase your rating.


Tip! Use the Heatmaps to see where your park needs more Scenery coverage.
tutorialscreen_image_shopmenus_1: A food and drink shop menu. Yum!
tutorialscreen_image_souvenirshopsluxury: The happier your guests, the more they'll want to buy Souvenir items like balloons or pool toys. Keeping guests happy pays off!
There are multiple souvenir shops that you can place down to help guests satisfy the Souvenir need.
Guests love to show off their souvenirs and will walk around the park proudly flaunting them.
tutorialscreen_image_staffbreaks_1: This staff member has a Break Block in their Schedule. Time for a well-deserved rest!
tutorialscreen_image_staffbreaks_3: The capacity of your Staff Building defines how many staff members can have a break at the same time.
tutorialscreen_image_staffhiringfiring_1: A park can't be run without hiring the right staff.
tutorialscreen_image_staffhiringfiring_2: Sometimes you'll need to fire a staff member, which can be done in their InfoPanel.
tutorialscreen_image_staffhiringfiring_3: ... Or in the Staff Management screen.
tutorialscreen_image_staffjanitors_2: Cleaning up rubbish to make your park look great.
tutorialscreen_image_staffjanitors_3: Clearing bins so they don't overflow.
tutorialscreen_image_staffjanitors_4: On their way to cleaning a toilet, with a spring in their step.
tutorialscreen_image_stafflifeguards_1: The pool is under the cool gaze of a Lifeguard.
tutorialscreen_image_stafflifeguards_2: This Lifeguard has spotted panicking guests!
tutorialscreen_image_stafflifeguards_3: The Lifeguard rushes over to help the guests...
tutorialscreen_image_staffmechanics_1: Your Mechanics roam your park, ready to spring into action if they spot something they can work on.
tutorialscreen_image_staffmechanics_2: Mechanics are experts at repairing rides.
tutorialscreen_image_staffmechanics_3: Transport rides need servicing now and then to keep them running.
tutorialscreen_image_staffmechanics_4: A Mechanic hard at work refurbishing a coaster.
tutorialscreen_image_staffmechanics_5: A Mechanic conducting some important Research in a Mechanic Workshop.
tutorialscreen_image_staffmorale_1: You can quickly check a staff member's Morale in their InfoPanel.
tutorialscreen_image_staffmorale_2: Now that's a happy staff member.
tutorialscreen_image_staffmorale_3: This staff member isn't happy at all!
tutorialscreen_image_staffpay_1: You can quickly check a staff member's pay in their InfoPanel.
tutorialscreen_image_staffpay_2: Staff Pay Scale affects Morale in different ways.
tutorialscreen_image_staffpay_3: A projection of Staff Pay at the current tier.
tutorialscreen_image_staffpoolsafety_1: You can't spell "fun" without "safety"! Okay, you can, but it's still important to keep your Pool Area Safety high to maximise your guest's enjoyment.
tutorialscreen_image_staffpoolsafety_2: A majestic Lifeguard in their natural habitat: ensuring the pool is as safe as safe can be.
tutorialscreen_image_staffpoolsafety_3: An empty Lifeguard Chair's info panel. Uh oh, looks like there's 0% Pool Area Safety!
tutorialscreen_image_staffrideattendants: A Ride Attendant at work on a Coaster...
tutorialscreen_image_staffscheduleadvanc: Work Blocks are when your workers will... work! Don't forget that advanced menu.
Break Blocks are a chance for workers to take a break and put their feet up.
Block Override lets you briefly change a staff member's schedule.
tutorialscreen_image_stampbuilder_1: The Stamp Builder lets you place precise shapes that snap together easily.
tutorialscreen_image_stampbuilder_2: Stamps can be scaled to quickly make larger shapes.
tutorialscreen_image_stampbuilder_3:
These can be intersected with the Merge setting to build large paved areas, like plazas, in just a few actions!


Rides can also be placed directly on top of paths and plazas.
tutorialscreen_image_surplus_1:
Surplus is the cumulative money a guest would have paid if you charged a higher price.

In other words, it's the extra money in their pocket that they would have been willing to spend already.
tutorialscreen_image_surplus_2: Guests will use their Surplus to make up the difference if you charge more than they would usually be willing to pay.
tutorialscreen_image_surplus_3: Guest Price Tolerance is how much more than the Bargain Price a guest is willing to pay.
tutorialscreen_image_terrainbrush_1: The Sculpting Brush is used to change the shape of terrain.
tutorialscreen_image_terrainbrush_2: The Painting Brush is used to change the texture of terrain.
tutorialscreen_image_terrainbrush_3: The Water Tool is used to add or remove water.
tutorialscreen_image_timepark_1: A quick glance at the time in your park.
tutorialscreen_image_timepark_2: Guests entering the park, brimming with excitement.
tutorialscreen_image_timepark_3: Guests enjoying a cooling pool in the sunshine.
tutorialscreen_image_water_1: Flumes and pools will get dirty as your guests continue to use them.
tutorialscreen_image_water_2: To clean them, make sure that your flumes are covered by an active Water Filter. Filters keep all water in their effective radius nice and clean!
tutorialscreen_image_water_3: To do this, connect Pumps and Filters together using Pipes. Your park entrance will provide water to get you started.
tutorialscreen_image_wavepool_1: Wave Machine objects can be found in the Pool Extras menu, and can be placed along the wall of a pool.
tutorialscreen_image_wavepool_2: Waves are generated up to the wall height of the pool, and can stack with other waves as long as the pool section is deep enough.
tutorialscreen_image_wavepool_3: Wave machines increase the prestige of a pool, attracting more guests to swim and play in the waves.
tutorialscreen_image_workshop_1: The diversity of Creations goes from the craziest coasters to the most outrageous parks and the simplest decorations.
tutorialscreen_image_workshop_2: Each Creation is different but you can find the best thanks to their ratings and number of subscribers.
